tldr: This quarter, the cohort demonstrated a clear rotation toward semiconductor infrastructure and financial data moats while actively trimming select mega-cap tech and consumer discretionary names. Despite mixed flows in Amazon and Alphabet, the aggressive selling of Microsoft and UnitedHealth suggests a desire to lock in gains from previous market leaders. Meanwhile, broad initiations in semiconductor ETFs and individual chipmakers highlight a continued, targeted bet on AI hardware over software, alongside a hunt for value in niche media and industrial equipment.

themes.heading: Leaning Into AI Hardware & Semiconductors
themes.detail: Managers aggressively initiated and added to semiconductor exposure, preferring the picks-and-shovels hardware layer. This was evidenced by broad buying in a major semiconductor ETF and individual chipmakers.

themes.heading: Accumulating Financial Moats
themes.detail: The cohort showed strong conviction in payment networks and financial data providers, adding to wide-moat compounders with high pricing power.

themes.heading: Taking Profits in Software & Consumer Discretionary
themes.detail: Investors leaned out of previous market darlings, heavily trimming Microsoft and UnitedHealth, alongside consumer-facing names like Target, Chipotle, and MercadoLibre.

themes.heading: Initiating Niche Value Plays
themes.detail: A surprising consensus emerged around specific media and industrial equipment names, indicating a hunt for value and cyclical exposure outside of the mega-cap tech space.
